title: anthyllis montana subsp. atropurpurea
intro: Anthyllis montana subsp. atropurpurea, also known as alpine kidney vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family that is native to mountainous regions in Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of alpine kidney vetch include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress, making it a valuable herbal remedy for promoting overall well-being. Alpine kidney vetch can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal creams and ointments to support skin health and wound care.
